name: Do my baseboards need to be removed before new flooring is installed?
headline: Do my baseboards need to be removed before new flooring is installed?

mainEntity.name: Do my baseboards need to be removed before new flooring is installed?
mainEntity.text: Do my baseboards need to be removed before new flooring is installed?

acceptedAnswer.text: In most homes, you should remove baseboards before installing new hard surface flooring like hardwood, laminate, or vinyl plank. Taking off the baseboards lets the installer leave the proper expansion gap between the floor and the wall, then cover that gap neatly when the baseboards go back on. Floating floors especially need this gap so the planks can expand and contract slightly with changes in temperature and humidity. If you leave the baseboards in place, the installer usually has to add quarter-round or shoe molding in front of them, which some people don’t like the look of. If you remove the baseboards yourself, gently score the caulk line with a utility knife, pry them off with a flat bar, and label each piece so you know where it goes later. Expect some paint touch-ups and possibly new caulk when they are reinstalled. Be careful not to damage the drywall. In some cases, such as when walls are very fragile or the trim is historic, an installer may recommend leaving baseboards and using additional molding instead. Talk about this before the job starts so everyone agrees on the look and cost. A common mistake is assuming baseboard work is included and then being surprised by extra charges or unfinished trim.
